tool_args.label: 14th Street Transit & Truck Priority Pilot Project Quarterly Report Winter 2020

payload.source_title: 14th Street Transit & Truck Priority Pilot Project Quarterly Report Winter 2020
payload.description: Quarterly monitoring report for the 14th Street Transit & Truck Priority Pilot Project covering data collected January/February 2020, before COVID-19 PAUSE order. Reports on bus operations, vehicle travel times, vehicle speeds, vehicle volumes, bike volumes, Citi Bike ridership, safety/crash data, enforcement, and community feedback.

payload.project_name: 14th Street Transit & Truck Priority Pilot Project
payload.description: Implemented by NYCDOT in October 2019, aiming to improve operations of the M14 A and M14 D Select Bus Service (M14 A/D SBS), increase safety on this Vision Zero Priority Corridor, and maintain the street as an important truck route. From 6 AM–10 PM, only buses, trucks, and emergency vehicles allowed on 14th Street from 3rd Avenue to 8th Avenue westbound and 9th Avenue to 3rd Avenue eastbound.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation
payload.role: implementer and monitor of TTP Pilot Project
data provider for bus performance and partner on TTP Pilot Project
monitoring consultant partnering with Traffic Databank and Public Works Partners

payload.description: Select Bus Service routes on 14th Street, part of the TTP Pilot Project. M14 A/D SBS services started in July 2019.

payload.limits: 3rd Avenue to 8th Avenue (westbound) and 9th Avenue to 3rd Avenue (eastbound)
payload.description: Transit & Truck Priority corridor on 14th Street restricted to buses, trucks, and emergency vehicles 6 AM–10 PM. Part of Vision Zero Priority Corridor.
14th Street TTP Pilot Project launched by NYCDOT
L Train reduced service between Manhattan and Brooklyn after 8 PM weekdays and all day weekends for tunnel work; in effect during data collection

New York State on PAUSE executive order issued by Governor Cuomo in response to COVID-19 pandemic, mandating closure of non-essential businesses and gatherings, causing data collection suspension for future reports
Added additional green time for westbound traffic at intersection of 13th Street and 5th Avenue
Added additional signage to 14th Street and along approaching avenues to inform drivers of new traffic regulations
Installed plastic delineators (bollards) along expanded pedestrian areas adjacent to Union Square
Installed bus boarding platforms (temporary sidewalk extensions at bus stops) along 14th Street to facilitate passenger access at key stops and provide additional waiting space
Installed benches at six locations along 14th Street for bus passengers and pedestrians
Implemented automated enforcement along 14th Street with both fixed position and bus-mounted cameras
Stationary cameras went into warning mode on launch day
Bus mounted (ABLE) cameras went into warning mode
Stationary cameras started sending violations 60 days after launch. NYPD stopped deploying Traffic Enforcement Agents to the corridor.
ABLE (bus-mounted camera) violations began
Weekday average travel time improvement combined both directions 3rd Avenue to 8th Avenue from January 2018 to January 2020
Combined both directions 3rd Avenue to 8th Avenue from January 2018 to January 2020

payload.claim_text: 49% of survey respondents identified reduced vehicular traffic on 14th Street as a top benefit of the project
payload.description: From public survey, respondents could choose up to 2 benefits, n=1,507

payload.claim_text: 31% of survey respondents identified quality of bus service as a top benefit of the project
payload.description: From public survey, respondents could choose up to 2 benefits

payload.claim_text: 33% of survey respondents said there have been no challenges to the success of the 14th Street busway
payload.description: From public survey, respondents could choose up to 2 challenges, n=1,507

payload.claim_text: 27% of survey respondents identified access to taxi/Uber/Lyft/Via as a top challenge
payload.description: From public survey, respondents could choose up to 2 challenges

payload.claim_text: Since the 14th Street Project changes, 75% of driving respondents said driving has become much more challenging
payload.description: From public survey, n=184 respondents who drive

payload.claim_text: 59% of bus takers said bus travel along 14th Street feels much faster since the project changes
payload.description: From public survey, n=824

payload.claim_text: 36% of respondents said their experience as a pedestrian crossing 14th Street feels much safer since the project changes
payload.description: From public survey, n=1,029

payload.claim_text: 6th Ave eastern-most lane between 14th Street and 15th Street occupied by construction activity during data collection
payload.description: Construction activity noted as context for study area conditions

payload.claim_text: 14th Street southern-most lane between Irving Place and 3rd Avenue occupied by construction activity during data collection
Street closures on January 18, 19, 25, 26 (weekends): 11th Street between 6th Avenue and 7th Avenue, and 15th Street between Union Square E and Irving Place

payload.gap_text: Data will not be gathered for future Quarterly Reports until travel conditions return to more typical activity levels due to COVID-19 pandemic
payload.missing_information: No future quarterly data collected after the Winter 2020 report due to COVID-19 PAUSE order
payload.description: Report states that data collection is suspended for future reports following the March 22, 2020 NY PAUSE executive order

payload.gap_text: Pre-implementation data from winter is not available for comparison for bicycle volumes
payload.missing_information: Winter pre-implementation bike volume data not available for before/after comparison
payload.description: Bicycle ridership is subject to seasonal factors; pre-implementation winter data not available
